The command modifier flag should not be zeroed when the mouse is pressed or raised. If it's an old code, when the second mouse click is pressed, the flag will be already zeroed.
This flag should be zeroed when the command key is raised.

I think we can have clever solution, e.g.
```
ntries++
for i in 0..ntries {
checkport change
sleep
}
```
ntries is reset if port changed or no errors. max of ntries can be set to 60

body:
@xxrlzzz Hi, I'm testing trackpad on win->macOS.

I find trackpad will not work correctly because macOS check if `ControlKey::Scroll` is in the modifiers.

https://github.com/rustdesk/rustdesk/blob/1ededa3476aab868364f6853706e91b7b0ad4e4c/src/server/input_service.rs#LL841C32-L841C32

`check_scroll_on_mac` is only compiled on macOS.

So, I have some questions:

1. Why `check_scroll_on_mac` is needed ? `mask & 7 == 4` is not enough to know whether current event is trackpad event?
2. I cann't understand the logic after `let btn = mask >> 3;`  in `check_scroll_on_mac`. Can you please explain or give a link of what you refered?
   I've revert the code to Commit `260c924`, but I cannot find where `0xff88`, `0x78`, `0x780000` are from.

https://github.com/rustdesk/rustdesk/blob/260c924010361137dd67be145e9d5c0850804768/src/flutter_ffi.rs#L840


`handler.send_mouse` in 
https://github.com/rustdesk/rustdesk/blob/260c924010361137dd67be145e9d5c0850804768/src/ui/remote.tis

body:
In German verbs are basically written in lower case:

Quote from https://de.wiktionary.org/wiki/Verzeichnis:Deutsch/Rechtschreibung/Gro%C3%9F-_und_Kleinschreibung_von_Verben/2006#.C3.9Cbersicht

Verben werden grundsätzlich kleingeschrieben.

    Beispiele: schreiben, tanzen, ich denke, wir haben gelesen ...

body:
Flutter's [latest release](https://docs.flutter.dev/get-started/install/linux) contains flutter@3.7.3 and dart@2.19.2

In [pubspec.yaml](https://github.com/rustdesk/rustdesk/blob/master/flutter/pubspec.yaml) file there's no upperbound for dart, refer to `environment.sdk` value. Therefore while resolving dependencies, flutter automatically created the upperbound in `pubspec.lock` file to `3.0.0` for dart i.e at `2.19.2`.

body:
@rustdesk 

To avoid this kind of problem with not English language  it's better to use specific strings and not use same string for more than one context.

Because in Italian/French/German (etc) a strings can be male/female/neutral.
Then to have a unique string can create errors in not English language

Ex. the English string "Auto" (Automatic) in italian could be "Automatico" (male) or "Automatica" (female) depend by context.

To avoid this issue in language file for this context use the string "Automatic codec" (it's specific).

Thanks.

author: awalon

body:
Highlevel: 
As mentioned by newly added debug messages auth- file will be extraxcted from Xorg process and for a relative path home path will be added.

If no HOME var was found the home folder for given user will be determined from system config (new function: get_user_home_by_name)  old logic just used hard coded  default pattern "/home/<username>" (now only if no system config was found)  which wasn't working for domain users (AD, samba, freeipa, ...) where an additional domain subfolder (/home/<domain>/<username>) needs to be added.

In addition detected file needs to exist, if not this loop won't detect any auth- file. So next step can detect a valid file.

Next step (Todo): Detect auth file by checking process tree (processes below Xorg) without need of predefined checks for Gname, XFCE, Plasma, ...). But as this was my first modifiaction using rust language, there was too less time for better solution.

author: m1ga

body:
Just thoughts. 

For constistency, some other "display"/"Anzeige" should changed to "Monitor" or "Bildschirm" then.

"Anzeigen" is more common as a verb, like:

```
("show_monitors_tip", "Monitore in der Symbolleiste anzeigen"),
```

```
("Show displays as individual windows", "Anzeigen als einzelne Fenster darstellen"),
```

- Monitore in eigenem Fenster anzeigen.
- Jeden Monitor in einem eigenen Fenster anzeigen.
- Alle Bildschirme in einem eigenen Fenster anzeigen.
- Ein eigenes Fenster für jeden Monitor [öffnen].
- Für jeden Bildschirm ein eigenes Fenster öffnen.

```
("elevated_switch_display_msg", "Wechseln Sie zur primären Anzeige, da die Mehrfachanzeige im erweiterten Modus nicht unterstützt wird."),
```

- Wechseln Sie zum primären Monitor/Bildschirm, …

```
("switch_display_elevated_connections_tip", "Das Umschalten auf eine nicht primäre Anzeige wird mit erhöhten Rechten nicht unterstützt, wenn mehrere Verbindungen bestehen. Bitte versuchen Sie es nach der Installation erneut, wenn Sie mehrere Anzeigen steuern möchten."),
```

- […] Umschalten auf einen sekundären / weiteren Monitor/Bildschirm wird […] mehrere Monitore / Bildschirme steuern […] 

```
("Display", "Anzeige"),
```

- Monitor / Bildschirm

body: socks5 proxy tested for nats connection?
author: rustdesk
rustdesk

body: The ability to establish a NATS MQ connection via a user-specified socks5 proxy has not yet been implemented. This is mainly because [nats.rs](https://github.com/nats-io/nats.rs) doesn't expose the necessary API. Instead, it just using `TcpStream::connect` directly ([async-nats/src/connector.rs](https://github.com/nats-io/nats.rs/blob/c661d2147ccbbfc25a32fad8e5b9c1cb9ca077df/async-nats/src/connector.rs#L291C27-L291C27)). We might need to fork our own version to replace it with other `dyn AsyncWrite + AsyncRead + Send + Unpin`, e.g., getting from `FramedStream`.
author: kaaass

body: We have to fork then.
author: rustdesk

body: OK. Let me check if I understand it right. This means we should consider the NATS server/cluster as a separate service, not just an extra port of a rendezvous server. Thus connecting to any of them would be enough.
author: kaaass

body: Yes, it is a seperate service, just like https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/products/iot-hub
author: rustdesk

body:
I got it. 

BTW, would it be acceptable if we send `NATS_SERVERS` in `RegisterPkResponse`? Since we could connect to MQ only after we get `mq_token`, IMO it is reasonable to also get `mq_servers` in the same way.
author: kaaass

body: README.md
author: farisei77

body: Seriously?
author: rustdesk

body: You added this in your PR https://github.com/rustdesk/rustdesk/pull/6628, and now remove it. So why?
author: rustdesk

body:
In #6628 I skipped supported resolutions on wayland as it doesn't work on wayland and it was making calls to `get_capturables` on loop and hence user prompts on loop.

But in this PR I have changed `get_capturables` so that it makes only one user prompt request no matter how many calls it gets. https://github.com/rustdesk/rustdesk/commit/eff2490081c48c4f251847de5024cb8d0e2a8c1e
So reverting #6628 enables the future possibility of supporting `supported resolutions` on wayland with only one user prompt. Currently, it is working the same with or without it.
